programme.title: The Love Witch
programme.desc: Elaine, a young witch, casts love spells that backfire, leaving hapless victims. When she meets her dream man, obsession drives her to madness and murder in this stylish, 60s-inspired thriller.

programme.title: The Flowers Of War
programme.desc: In 1937 China, during the second Sino-Japanese war, a mortician, John (Christian Bale) arrives at a Catholic church in Nanjing to prepare a priest for burial. Upon arrival he finds himself the lone adult among a group of convent girl students and prostitutes from a nearby brothel.

programme.title: Love Thing
programme.desc: A dirty campaign forces movie star Marvin to flee from reporters and fans. Coincidentally, he immerses himself in the cosmos of the feminist/queer off-theatre 3000.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Margaret Pomeranz interviews producer Robin Dalton and director John Schlesinger about Madam Sousatska, focusing on the casting and Shirley MacLaine's performance.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: Some of the biggest film stars in history rose to fame during the 'golden age' of Hollywood, in the early and mid-20th century. We profile some of these stars who became household names.

programme.title: Thirteen Days
programme.desc: In October 1962, President John F. Kennedy learns about the Soviet Union's plan of placing nuclear weapons in Cuba. These weapons could wipe out most of the eastern and southern United States in minutes if they become operational. Kennedy and his advisors must come up with a plan of action against the Soviets.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Margaret Pomeranz interviews Dustin Hoffman, Barry Levinson and Tom Cruise about Rain Man and its characters, with discussion of Sweet Hearts Dance, Tucker, Dear America, Green Tea and Cherry Ripe.

programme.title: Superheroes
programme.desc: The story of a young couple who falls in love and struggles to keep its relationship alive going through tenderness and fights, lies and emotions, secrets and cheatings. A sentimental dramedy about couples and the effect of time passing on their relationships, posing a simple but profound question: what superpowers you need to have, to love each other your whole life?

programme.title: Good Morning Vietnam
programme.desc: In 1965 Saigon, irreverent radio DJ Adrian Cronauer shakes up Armed Forces Radio with his wild humor, clashing with military censors while confronting the realities of the Vietnam War.

programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: After witnessing Germany's rapidly advancing air power, visionary aviator R.J. Mitchell becomes determined to design a fighter that could defend Britain. Battling illness, he pushes himself to create a groundbreaking aircraft as war looms.

programme.title: Mia & The White Lion
programme.desc: Mia moves to South Africa with her family to manage a lion farm and develops a special bond with Charlie, a white lion cub; however, things change when she uncovers her father's secret.

programme.title: One of Our Aircraft Is Missing
programme.desc: On its way back from a raid on the city of Stuttgart, Germany, a British bomber is shot down over Nazi-held Holland. Parachuting into Dutch farmlands under cover of darkness, the six-member crew connects with members of the local resistance, who shelter the Brits from their Nazi inquisitors as they make their way towards freedom.

programme.title: Letters from Iwo Jima
programme.desc: As the Americans approach, the Japanese defenders dig deep into the volcanic earth of Iwo Jima. Some want to be heroes, others just want to see their wives.

programme.title: Rendition
programme.desc: Isabella El-Ibrahimi, the wife of an Egyptian-born engineer, tries desperately to track down her husband after he disappears on an international flight. Meanwhile, a CIA analyst's world spins out of control as he witnesses the engineer's interrogation and torture at the hands of the secret police.

programme.title: The Love Witch
programme.desc: Elaine, a young witch, casts love spells that backfire, leaving hapless victims. When she meets her dream man, obsession drives her to madness and murder in this stylish, 60s-inspired thriller.

programme.title: Southpaw
programme.desc: After losing everything to a senseless tragedy, a boxer turns to a retired fighter for guidance as he struggles toward redemption.

programme.title: Mia & The White Lion
programme.desc: Mia moves to South Africa with her family to manage a lion farm and develops a special bond with Charlie, a white lion cub; however, things change when she uncovers her father's secret.

programme.title: One of Our Aircraft Is Missing
programme.desc: On its way back from a raid on the city of Stuttgart, Germany, a British bomber is shot down over Nazi-held Holland. Parachuting into Dutch farmlands under cover of darkness, the six-member crew connects with members of the local resistance, who shelter the Brits from their Nazi inquisitors as they make their way towards freedom.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: Some of the biggest film stars in history rose to fame during the 'golden age' of Hollywood, in the early and mid-20th century. We profile some of these stars who became household names.

programme.title: Southpaw
programme.desc: After losing everything to a senseless tragedy, a boxer turns to a retired fighter for guidance as he struggles toward redemption.

programme.title: The Tunnel
programme.desc: In 1961 Berlin, athlete Harry escapes East Germany but his sister Lotte is trapped. Leads a daring tunnel-digging rescue, racing against guards in a tense fight for freedom.

programme.title: Thirteen Days
programme.desc: In October 1962, President John F. Kennedy learns about the Soviet Union's plan of placing nuclear weapons in Cuba. These weapons could wipe out most of the eastern and southern United States in minutes if they become operational. Kennedy and his advisors must come up with a plan of action against the Soviets.

programme.title: The Red Turtle
programme.desc: Gorgeously animated, this wordless story about a stranded castaway pays tribute to what's most important in life: companionship, love, family, and the stewardship of nature.

programme.title: The Caine Mutiny
programme.desc: Set aboard fictional minesweeper, the Caine, during World War II, rich kid Willis Seward Keith (Robert Francis) reports for duty fresh out of officer training school. He is shocked to discover that the ship's captain, Lt. Commander DeVriess (Tom Tully), has let discipline on the ship slide and allows his men to exhibit a casual attitude to their duties. What Keith fails to notice, however, is that the crew of the Caine are in fact excellent at their jobs. But life aboard changes drastically when DeVriess is replaced by veteran no-nonsense captain, Queeg (Humphrey Bogart).

programme.title: Heartbreak Ridge
programme.desc: Hard-nosed, hard-living Marine Gunnery Sergeant Tom Highway clashes with his superiors and his ex-wife as he takes command of a spoiled recon platoon with a bad attitude.

programme.title: A Midnight Clear
programme.desc: December 1944, Ardennes Forest: Six US soldiers occupy a house for observation, but the expected German advance is met with surprisingly friendly troops.

programme.title: Letters from Iwo Jima
programme.desc: As the Americans approach, the Japanese defenders dig deep into the volcanic earth of Iwo Jima. Some want to be heroes, others just want to see their wives.

programme.title: Moving
programme.desc: Renko, a girl in the sixth grade, is at first unperturbed by her parents' decision to separate. With the companionship of her boyfriend, Minoru, and Sally, a classmate in the same boat, Renko gradually realises the practical implications of divorce, and tries repeatedly to get her parents to reconcile. An old man teaches her about the need to forget the past.
